name: Indian Oil Marketing (IOC/BPCL/HPCL)
summary: Day 89 (~48h window — NO brief May 26). **NO FIFTH HIKE — THE PASS-THROUGH PAUSED/PLATEAUED AFTER FOUR HIKES; THE MAY 25 FOURTH HIKE STANDS, BUT DELHI PETROL HAS NOW CROSSED ₹100.** No new OMC price action this window: the cumulative ~₹7.5/L since May 15 (May 15 +₹3; May 19 +~90p; May 23 +~87-91p; May 25 +₹2.61-2.71, the largest) is the operative total. Crucially, the price level keeps grinding higher as the May 25 hike fully propagates city-by-city: **Delhi petrol has crossed ₹100 to ₹102.12 (diesel ₹95.20)**, Mumbai petrol ₹111.21 (Goodreturns, India.com, BusinessToday May 27). The April 27 fuel-pass-through trigger stays `active_fired` — but with NO new hike it is NOT firing harder; the pause is itself the signal (the cadence broke to economics-led on May 25, and a plateau after four hikes is consistent with the OMCs having recovered enough of the under-recovery envelope for now). **The oil backdrop eased slightly but stays out-of-range**: operative Brent rebounded to ~$99 (CNBC: Brent +3.58% to $99.58 May 26 on the strikes; ~-14% MoM on net tail-premium de-pricing) and USD/INR gave back its firming to ₹95.68 (rupee weaker) — net, the import-bill / under-recovery envelope is marginally better than the May peak but $99 Brent + ₹95.68 still sits above the ~$85-90 break-even ceiling at which OMC marketing economics turn break-even. (NOTE: operative Brent ~$99, NOT the yfinance -6.8%/$92.78 roll artifact.) PRIOR (May 25): Day 87 (Monday May 25). **FOURTH PETROL/DIESEL HIKE LANDED MONDAY MAY 25: +₹2.61-2.71/LITRE — THE LARGEST SINGLE HIKE OF THE SEQUENCE — BREAKING THE 'SATURDAY-ONLY' CADENCE. Sequence: May 15 +₹3; May 19 +~90p; May 23 +~87-91p; May 25 +₹2.61-2.71 = CUMULATIVE ~₹7.5/L SINCE MAY 15. Mumbai petrol ₹111.21 / diesel ₹97.83; Kolkata petrol ₹113.51 / diesel ₹99.82; Chennai petrol ₹107.77 (India TV News, Goodreturns May 25). A LARGE MONDAY HIKE ON A US/UK MARKET HOLIDAY, THE DAY BEFORE THE RBI SWAP AUCTION AND THE QUAD, MATERIALLY REVISES THE 'POLITICAL-TOLERANCE CADENCE' READ — OMC PASS-THROUGH IS NOW TRACKING COST-RECOVERY / REFINING-MARGIN PRESSURE AND A WEAK RUPEE MORE THAN A POLITICAL CALENDAR. THE APRIL 27 FUEL-PASS-THROUGH TRIGGER STAYS `active_fired` AND IS FIRING HARDER.** Crucially, the relief context cuts the other way for the equity read: Brent slid below $100 Monday (US-Iran de-escalation hopes) and USD/INR firmed to ₹95.22, which modestly eases the OMC import-bill / under-recovery envelope at the margin — but $100 Brent + ₹95.22 still leaves marketing margins structurally compressed vs the ~$85-90 Brent ceiling at which OMC economics turn break-even. The largest single hike of the cascade landing ON a deal-relief day is the cleanest evidence the pass-through is cost-recovery-driven, not opportunistic. PRIOR (May 24): **NO FOURTH OMC HIKE SUNDAY MAY 24 — CUMULATIVE PATTERN (₹4.74 PETROL + ₹4.82 DIESEL ACROSS MAY 15-19-23) HOLDS. GOODRETURNS MAY 24 CONFIRMS DELHI PETROL ₹99.51, KOLKATA >₹110, MUMBAI >₹108. BUSINESS STANDARD MAY 20: ₹3 CUMULATIVE HIKE REDUCED OMC DAILY LOSSES ~25% FROM ~₹1,000 CR/DAY TO ~₹750 CR/DAY. THE SATURDAY-ONLY-NO-SUNDAY PATTERN SHOWS OMC PACE CALIBRATED TO POLITICAL TOLERANCE. APRIL 27 FUEL-PASS-THROUGH TRIGGER REMAINS ACTIVE_FIRED DAY 5 OF 30. POLITICAL-ECONOMIC TRANSMISSION CHANNEL IS OPERATIONALLY ACTIVE. GOVERNMENT POLICY BACKDROP: MARCH 27 SPECIAL ADDITIONAL EXCISE DUTY REDUCTION ESTIMATED TO COST ~₹30,000 CR IN CURRENT FISCAL.** Brent flat at $103.54 weekend close = OMC relief envelope still deeply OUT-OF-RANGE. **The new brent-crude trigger 'Brent breaks $100 sustained 3+ sessions'** is the cleanest leading indicator for OMC relief envelope: if fired, OMC compressed-spread trade unwinds toward Q3 break-even. PRIOR (May 23): THIRD PETROL/DIESEL HIKE LANDED MAY 23 (IOC/BPCL/HPCL): 87 PAISE/LITRE PETROL + DIESEL CORRESPONDINGLY, CUMULATIVE ₹4.74/L PETROL + ₹4.82/L DIESEL ACROSS MAY 15, 19, 23. PETROL CROSSED ₹99.51 DELHI, ₹110 KOLKATA, ₹108 MUMBAI. FIRST SUSTAINED HIKE PATTERN IN 76 DAYS. APRIL 27 FUEL-PASS-THROUGH TRIGGER NOW IN CONFIRMED CASCADE MODE DAY 4 OF 30.** Brent flat at $103-104 weekend close = OMC relief envelope still deeply OUT-OF-RANGE; The Week analysis: 'might just be the beginning' given India's 85%+ crude import dependency and Indian crude basket averaging $113-114/bbl post-war vs $69 pre-war. Business Today framed this as the first sustained petrol/diesel hike pattern in 76 days. Hardeep Puri's ₹1,000 cr/day PSU OMC under-recovery quantification (May 13) is operative through the deal-anticipation window; total under-recovery on track ₹1.98 lakh cr. The three-hikes-in-8-days pattern is **the inflation transmission channel that hits Indian households directly, drives political backlash, feeds back into RBI policy via core CPI**. The Modi-MBZ MoUs (May 15 IndianOil-ADNOC LPG framework + Hormuz-bypass at Fujairah) provide structural hedge but DON'T resolve current pricing/under-recovery dynamics. **The brent-crude trigger 'Brent breaks $100 sustained 3+ sessions' (watching Day 0 of 3)** is the cleanest leading indicator for OMC relief envelope: if fired, OMC compressed-spread trade unwinds toward Q3 break-even. PRIOR (May 22): **BRENT $104.52 -5.3% CUMULATIVE WINDOW; WTI BROKE $100 TO $99.50. OMC RELIEF ENVELOPE PARTIALLY REASSERTS BUT STRUCTURAL DISRUPTION STILL DOMINANT — $104.52 BRENT STILL 43% ABOVE $73 BASELINE. THE FUEL-PASS-THROUGH TRIGGER FIRED MAY 19 REMAINS `active_fired` WITH NO FURTHER HIKES REPORTED.** Hardeep Puri's ₹1,000 cr/day PSU OMC under-recovery quantification (May 13) is operative through the deal-anticipation window; total under-recovery on track ₹1.98 lakh cr. **USD/INR retraced from intra-week record low ₹97.15 to ₹95.69 close on ~$5B RBI intervention** — provides modest CAD-pressure relief on PSU OMCs, but the structural import-bill drain at $104.52 + ₹95.69 still adds ~$30-40B annualised vs pre-crisis. **Modi-MBZ MoUs from May 15 (IndianOil-ADNOC LPG framework + ISPRL-ADNOC 30 mn bbl + Indian crude at Fujairah outside Hormuz) provide structural Hormuz-bypass hedge** but DON'T resolve current pricing/under-recovery dynamics. **The MOU framework is the structural relief catalyst**: requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + Hormuz operational reopening — neither imminent. **The new brent-crude trigger 'Brent breaks $100 sustained 3+ sessions' (added May 22, watching Day 0 of 3)** is the cleanest leading indicator for OMC relief envelope: if fired, Brent into JPM $60-90 band reopens, OMC compressed-spread trade unwinds toward Q3 break-even. **Camp B framing intact**: even with Brent -5.3% and INR retracement, the underlying OMC under-recovery does not reverse — RBI intervention caps INR but cannot make OMC marketing margins economic at Brent $104 + Hormuz physically constrained. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: tail-risk de-pricing component (~25%) helps modestly; structural component (~75%) requires durable Hormuz reopening (Baker Hughes 2H26 call). PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **BRENT $111.47 +2.0% MONDAY (StanChart $130-145 path activates — 2-session sustain rule met) — OMC RELIEF ENVELOPE NOW DEEPLY OUT-OF-RANGE; STRUCTURAL DISRUPTION FRAME REASSERTING.** Hardeep Puri's ₹1,000 cr/day PSU OMC under-recovery quantification (May 13) is operative; total under-recovery on track ₹1.98 lakh cr. **A6 USD/INR ₹96.29 record low (Day 6 of 5-7 trigger fires)** compounds the import-bill pressure on PSU OMCs: every $1 above ~$83 baseline adds ~$1.6-2B/yr to India's import bill; at $111 + ₹96.29, incremental CAD drain ~$45-55B annualised vs pre-crisis. **B2 Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04% Monday** confirms broader risk-off; PSU OMC equity overhang persists. Modi-MBZ MoUs from May 15 (IndianOil-ADNOC LPG framework + ISPRL-ADNOC 30 mn bbl + Indian crude at Fujairah outside Hormuz) provide structural Hormuz-bypass hedge but DON'T resolve current pricing/under-recovery dynamics. Nationwide fuel price hike reported May 15 — politically-explosive pass-through. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + Hormuz operational reopening — neither imminent. **PRIOR (May 16): BRENT $109.26 +3.3% 1D / WTI $105.42 +4.2% — OMC RELIEF ENVELOPE NOW FULLY OUT-OF-RANGE; structural-disruption frame reasserting through WTI +11% weekly. Hardeep Puri ₹1,000 cr/day PSU OMC under-recovery (₹1.98 lakh cr on track) is the operative fiscal-pressure framing.** **A4 MODI-MBZ MoUs SIGNED MAY 15 with long-term LPG SALE-AND-PURCHASE FRAMEWORK BETWEEN INDIANOIL AND ADNOC** (CONFIRMED — Business Standard, The Tribune, The Wire) = structural India-LPG architecture cemented; provides operational hedge against Hormuz disruption for IOC specifically. **B7 nationwide fuel price hike (reported May 15)** — politically-explosive pass-through that yesterday's brief flagged as trigger condition is firing. PSU OMC equity overhang persists; Reliance refining-margin tailwind diverges from OMC marketing-margin compression. **The MOU framework as structural relief catalyst remains operative**: would require Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + Hormuz operational reopening. Neither imminent. PRIOR (May 7): **OMC RELIEF ENVELOPE NOW IN-RANGE: BRENT AT $101.95 MAY 7 MORNING (-7% MAY 6 ON MOU SCOOP); WORLD BANK $86 AVERAGE FOR 2026 + GOLDMAN Q2 $90 = INSTITUTIONAL FORECAST BAND BELOW INDIAN OMC LOSS-THRESHOLD.** Brent at $101 compresses CAD pressure significantly vs $115 — but OMCs remain capped because (1) Hormuz still operationally closed (5-6 vessels/day vs 138 norm; Trump pause preserved blockade explicitly); (2) $101 still above India's structural-loss-threshold (~$85-90 ceiling without pump-price pass-through); (3) Iran's first unconditional Hormuz reopening offer of cycle is REPORTED (PBS/AP/Al Jazeera/IRNA) but NOT yet operationally translated. The OMC forced-choice 30-day window (started ~Apr 13) is at Day ~24, expiring ~May 27. RBI special-credit-facility for OMC USD-demand REMAINS ACTIVE. CHABAHAR remains closed (US waiver expired April 26). **The MOU framework is the structural relief catalyst:** if signed within 14 days (by May 21) AND Hormuz operationally reopens (transit volume above 30/day for 3 consecutive days), OMC compressed-spread trade unwinds — Brent toward $86-90 (Goldman/World Bank) materially improves IOC/BPCL/HPCL margin profile. Watch Araghchi-India May 7-8 for any signal on India-Iran rupee-settlement oil track restart (would re-open the Chabahar escape hatch).

current.brent_futures: operative ~$99/bbl May 27 (CNBC: Brent +3.58% to $99.58 May 26 on the strikes; intraday range ~$94-107 on headline risk; ~-14% MoM net tail-premium de-pricing) — marginal OMC import-bill relief but still above the ~$85-90 break-even ceiling. (yfinance prints $92.78/-6.8% = roll/settlement ARTIFACT, NOT a real 1D move)
current.india_crude_basket: $113-114/bbl post-war average vs February ~$69.01 — pass-through pressure structural
current.structural_losses: ~₹20/L petrol + ~₹100/L diesel theoretical gross; pre-hike pump prices frozen ~4 years — now passing through (4 hikes May 15-25, cumulative ~₹7.5/L; NO fifth hike as of May 27 — pass-through paused/plateaued)
current.retail_fuel_prices: NO FIFTH HIKE this window — the May 25 fourth hike (+₹2.61-2.71/L, largest of sequence; cumulative ~₹7.5/L since May 15) stands and has fully propagated city-by-city: DELHI PETROL CROSSED ₹100 TO ₹102.12 (diesel ₹95.20), Mumbai petrol ₹111.21 (Goodreturns, India.com, BusinessToday May 27); Kolkata petrol ₹113.51 / diesel ₹99.82; Chennai petrol ₹107.77
current.rbi_special_credit_facility: ACTIVE ~2 weeks (since ~April 13-15) — RBI instructed IOC/HPCL/BPCL to curb spot USD buying and use special credit facilities for dollar needs (Reuters via MarketScreener India + ORF)
current.iranian_crude_supply: IOC Jaya 2M bbl + Reliance Felicity 2M bbl discharged before April 19 waiver expiry. No further Iranian purchases planned.
current.waiver_status: Iran waiver lapsed April 19. Russian 30-day waiver extension preserves ~1.9 mbpd Russian flow.
current.omc_usd_demand: ~$250-300M daily; now routed through RBI-administered credit windows rather than spot market
current.ppac_signal: PPAC publishing daily international crude rates — Economic Times read as preparation tell for fuel-price pass-through
current.monday_cash_open_expected: IOC/BPCL/HPCL +1.5-3% on diesel margin recovery (Branch 3 base case)
current.government_forced_choice: 30-day window (by 2026-05-27): (a) raise pump prices, (b) fiscal subsidy ~₹2,800 cr/week, (c) Iran-rupee oil track via Chabahar, (d) wait for Brent normalisation

active_triggers.condition: Government instructs OMCs to freeze fuel prices despite $110+ oil
OMC fuel-price pass-through announced OR Iran-rupee oil track via Chabahar restart within 30 days (by 2026-05-27)
active_triggers.activation_note: Pass-through fired with the May 15-19 hikes inside the 30-day window. By May 25 it has fired FOUR times (May 15 +₹3; May 19 +~90p; May 23 +~87-91p; May 25 +₹2.61-2.71, the largest), cumulative ~₹7.5/L.
active_triggers.update_2026-05-25: Now firing HARDER — fourth hike May 25 is the largest single hike and breaks the Saturday-only cadence (a big Monday hike on a US/UK holiday before the RBI swap auction + Quad), reading as cost-recovery/refining-margin + weak-rupee pass-through, not a political calendar.
active_triggers.update_2026-05-27: PAUSE/PLATEAU — NO fifth hike over the May 26-27 window. The trigger stays `active_fired` but is NOT firing harder (no new action). The May 25 fourth hike fully propagated — Delhi petrol crossed ₹100 to ₹102.12 (diesel ₹95.20). A plateau after four hikes + the marginal oil easing (Brent ~$99) is consistent with OMCs having recovered enough under-recovery for now; watch for whether the pause holds or a fifth hike resumes the cadence.

recent_signals.headline: **NO FIFTH HIKE — THE PASS-THROUGH PAUSED AFTER FOUR HIKES; THE MAY 25 FOURTH HIKE STANDS AND DELHI PETROL HAS NOW CROSSED ₹100 TO ₹102.12.** Over the ~48h window (no brief May 26) there was no new OMC price action: the cumulative ~₹7.5/L since May 15 (May 15 +₹3; May 19 +~90p; May 23 +~87-91p; May 25 +₹2.61-2.71, the largest) remains the operative total. The May 25 hike fully propagated city-by-city — **Delhi petrol crossed ₹100 to ₹102.12 (diesel ₹95.20)**, Mumbai petrol ₹111.21 (Goodreturns, India.com, BusinessToday May 27). **The trigger stays `active_fired` but is NOT firing harder** — no new action this window; the pause is itself the signal. A plateau after four hikes is consistent with the cadence having broken to economics-led on May 25 and OMCs having recovered enough of the under-recovery envelope for now. **The oil backdrop eased marginally but stays out-of-range**: operative Brent rebounded to ~$99 (CNBC: Brent +3.58% to $99.58 May 26 on the US strikes; ~-14% MoM net tail-premium de-pricing) and USD/INR gave back its firming to ₹95.68 (rupee weaker) — net, the import-bill / under-recovery envelope is marginally better than the May peak but $99 Brent + ₹95.68 still sits above the ~$85-90 break-even ceiling. Hardeep Puri's ~₹1,000 cr/day under-recovery quantification (May 13) remains the operative fiscal framing; the cumulative ₹7.5/L is the inflation-transmission channel feeding core CPI. **Watch**: whether the pause holds or a fifth hike resumes the cadence. (NOTE: operative Brent ~$99, NOT the yfinance -6.8%/$92.78 roll artifact.)
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-27 A9 (no fifth hike; Delhi petrol crossed ₹100 to ₹102.12, diesel ₹95.20, Mumbai ₹111.21)
Markets dossier 2026-05-27 C1 (operative Brent ~$99, +3.58% to $99.58 May 26; ~-14% MoM), A (USD/INR ₹95.68 weaker), C2 (India fuel pass-through now structural)
Goodreturns + India.com + BusinessToday May 27 (city fuel prices); CNBC 'Brent oil jumps more than 3%' May 26
recent_signals.verification: No fifth hike + cumulative ~₹7.5/L stands = CONFIRMED non-event (intel A9, negative confirmation); Delhi petrol ₹102.12 / diesel ₹95.20 / Mumbai ₹111.21 = CONFIRMED city pricing (Goodreturns + India.com + BusinessToday); operative Brent ~$99 rebound on the strikes = CONFIRMED (CNBC + markets dossier C1; yfinance $92.78/-6.8% is a roll/settlement artifact, NOT a real 1D move); marginal-relief-still-above-breakeven = CONFIRMED analytical observation.

recent_signals.headline: **FOURTH OMC HIKE +₹2.61-2.71/L MAY 25 — LARGEST OF THE SEQUENCE; PATTERN-BREAK TO A COST-RECOVERY CADENCE.** OMCs (IOC/BPCL/HPCL, ~90% of retail) raised petrol+diesel by ₹2.61-2.71/litre Monday May 25 — the fourth increase in under two weeks and the LARGEST single hike of the cascade (May 15 +₹3; May 19 +~90p; May 23 +~87-91p; May 25 +₹2.61-2.71), cumulative ~₹7.5/L since revisions resumed May 15. Prices: Mumbai petrol ₹111.21 / diesel ₹97.83; Kolkata petrol ₹113.51 / diesel ₹99.82; Chennai petrol ₹107.77 (India TV News, Goodreturns May 25). **The mechanism / regime read**: a large Monday hike landing on a US/UK market holiday, the day before the RBI swap auction and the Quad, materially revises the prior 'Saturday-only, calibrated-to-political-tolerance' thesis. OMC pass-through is now tracking cost recovery / refining-margin pressure and a weak rupee more than a political-tolerance calendar — the seal is fully broken and the cadence is now economics-led. **Counter-current**: this landed on a deal-relief day (Brent slid below $100, USD/INR firmed to ₹95.22), which modestly eases the import-bill/under-recovery envelope at the margin — but $100 Brent + ₹95.22 still sits above the ~$85-90 break-even ceiling, so marketing margins stay structurally compressed. That the LARGEST hike came on a relief day is itself the cleanest confirmation the pass-through is cost-recovery-driven, not opportunistic. Hardeep Puri's ~₹1,000 cr/day under-recovery quantification (May 13) remains operative; the cumulative ₹7.5/L is the inflation-transmission channel feeding core CPI and compressing RBI optionality. The April 27 fuel-pass-through trigger stays `active_fired`, firing harder.
sources: India TV News May 25 (₹2.61-2.71 hike, cumulative ₹7.5, city prices)
Intel dossier 2026-05-25 A10; Markets dossier C2
recent_signals.verification: Fourth fuel hike ₹2.61-2.71/L + cumulative ₹7.5 + city prices = CONFIRMED ACTION (India TV News + Goodreturns multi-source); pattern-break to cost-recovery cadence = CONFIRMED analytical read (timing on US/UK holiday before swap auction + Quad); Brent sub-$100 marginal relief vs break-even = CONFIRMED analytical observation.

recent_signals.headline: **OMC PASS-THROUGH ACTIVATED — TWO ~90 PAISE PETROL/DIESEL HIKES IN A WEEK; APRIL 27 FUEL-PASS-THROUGH TRIGGER FIRES INSIDE 30-DAY WINDOW.** The Modi government chose to break the political seal — pre-2024-election it deferred price hikes for 5 quarters; choosing TWO hikes in a week now signals fiscal patience is exhausted. **Mechanism**: India imports ~85% of crude; sustained Brent $109-111 + USD/INR ₹96 + the May 1 weekly-reserves drawdown of $7.79B forced OMCs to either continue absorbing the import-bill shock (IOC/BPCL/HPCL Q4 margin destruction, 3 weeks of stock-price punishment of OMCs has been pricing this) or pass it through to consumers. **The government chose passthrough — margin-protective for OMCs but politically explosive.** Pre-election the deferral was political; the pass-through now is policy. **Follow-on**: 90 paise increase = ~0.85% inflation contribution if sustained; food/transport inflation will follow with 2-3 week lag.
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A4; Markets dossier C6
recent_signals.verification: India fuel hike ~90 paise = CONFIRMED ACTION (WION + Goodreturns multi-source); second hike in a week = CONFIRMED; OMC pass-through choice = CONFIRMED operational/political signal.

historical_summaries.summary: 11 signals. Government slashed excise duty: petrol from ₹13 to ₹3/liter, diesel duty eliminated entirely (March 27, effective) | Oil at $109-112 keeps marketing margins deeply NEGATIVE for IOC, BPCL, HPCL | Indian crude basket at $120
